svg.text: Project Architecture: link URL shortener
Layered design with consumer-defined interfaces, adapter implementations, and cross-cutting middleware.
Composition root: cmd/linkd
Parses config, builds logger + trace handler, dials database, wires handlers and middleware, starts HTTP server.
Routes: POST /shorten, GET /r/{key}, /health
Delivery layer: rest
HTTP handlers, request decode/encode, redirects, error-to-status mapping.
Defines consumer interfaces:
- Shortener: Shorten(context.Context, link.Link) (link.Key, error)
- Resolver: Resolve(context.Context, link.Key) (link.Link, error)
Consumer-first contract: rest depends on behavior, not concrete storage type.
Domain layer: link
Core model and rules: Link, Key, Validate, Shorten.
Shared error taxonomy: ErrBadRequest, ErrConflict, ErrNotFound, ErrInternal.
Optional in-memory adapter: link.Shortener (same behavior surface).
Business rules are centralized and reused by all adapters.
Infrastructure adapter: sqlite
Dial(), schema bootstrap, SQL-backed Shortener/Resolve implementation.
Translates DB-specific errors to domain errors (e.g., PK violation -> conflict).
Persists and retrieves link records from SQLite.
Cross-cutting utilities: kit/*
hlog: request duration/status logging middleware.
traceid: request-scoped trace IDs + slog handler enrichment.
hio: small HTTP helpers (handler chaining, JSON decode, responder).
Applied at composition root so business code stays focused.
SQLite database (links table from schema.sql)
Solid arrow: runtime call dependency
Dashed arrow: shared contracts / utilities
Interfaces are implicit in Go (method set match)
